title: Aureline design-token, component-state, theme-support, accessibility-posture, and layer / scrim vocabulary export
description: Boundary schema for the design_token_export_manifest_record, the token_family_record, the component_state_record, the theme_support_row_record, the accessibility_posture_record, the layer_order_record, the scrim_token_record, and the token_export_audit_event_record frozen in docs/design/design_token_component_state_vocabulary.md. Every shell, docs / help, trust, onboarding, and durable-attention surface that mints tokens, component states, themes, accessibility postures, overlay layers, or scrim / opacity values reads one record per entity from this export. Raw bytes, raw asset payloads, raw rendered screenshots, and raw user content never cross this boundary; this schema carries ids, typed vocabulary, and small value packets only. The eventual design-system crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new token family, component state, theme, accessibility posture, layer order, scrim token, icon treatment, semantic status, trust visual state, density, audit-event id, or denial reason is additive-minor and requires a design_token_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

design_token_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this design-token / component-state / theme-support / layer-scrim vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / conformance reports / support bundles. Raw asset bytes, raw screenshots, raw rendered imagery, and raw user content never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

token_family_class.description: Frozen token-family vocabulary. Every design token belongs to exactly one family. Surfaces MUST NOT mint parallel families (e.g. no 'brand-v2-color' or 'shell-shadow-family'); new families arrive as an additive-minor schema bump.

component_state_class.description: Frozen component-state vocabulary. Every styled interactive surface maps exactly one of these states per addressable slot; a surface MAY combine them (e.g. focus + selected) but MUST NOT invent private state names (no 'busy', 'active-2', 'just-saved'). quiet_hours_held is the held / suppressed posture for durable-attention surfaces whose owner has declined a notification while preserving the row for later attention. restricted and policy_blocked remain separate axes: restricted names workspace-trust narrowing, policy_blocked names admin-policy narrowing; a surface that collapses them into one state is non-conforming.

theme_class.description: Frozen theme vocabulary. dark_reference is the reference theme; light_parity MUST be parity-quality and is never a legacy mode; high_contrast_dark and high_contrast_light are first-party modes. Admin policy MAY narrow the set a surface offers; it MAY NOT introduce a fifth theme without a schema bump.

accessibility_posture_class.description: Frozen accessibility / runtime motion posture vocabulary. motion_standard is the default. motion_reduced is the OS-level reduced-motion preference. motion_low_motion is the in-product low-motion mode layered on top of motion_reduced for users who want motion muted further than the OS floor. motion_power_saver is a runtime posture engaged when the device enters battery saver, thermal pressure, or an explicit low-power mode. motion_critical_hot_path is the engine-internal posture used when a frame budget is already committed; transient motion is suppressed regardless of the user's chosen motion posture. A surface MAY escalate from motion_standard to a more restrictive posture; it MAY NOT silently downgrade to a less restrictive posture once a more restrictive posture has engaged.

layer_order_class.description: Frozen layer / portal order vocabulary. Extensions and local features consume these tokens; hard-coded z-index values are non-conforming. Dialogs and security-critical sheets always render above menus and transient hovers; toasts never obscure the primary action area of an active blocking dialog; z_critical is reserved for trust, auth, or security-critical overlays that outrank every other layer.

scrim_class.description: Frozen scrim / overlay vocabulary. scrim_weak covers drawers and transient overlays; scrim_strong isolates blocking dialogs; overlay_dim_presentation dims the workspace beneath a presentation or follow-mode overlay without obscuring the focused interactive control (UI/UX Spec §19.2). Reduced opacity is NEVER the only indicator of disabled or blocked state; scrim tokens communicate depth and focus, not state.

density_class.description: Frozen density vocabulary. Density affects row heights, padding, tab density, sidebar spacing, and panel chrome. Density does NOT affect contrast, command semantics, focus visibility, or information architecture.

icon_treatment_class.description: Frozen icon / illustration treatment vocabulary. shell_stroke_standard is the default crisp shell icon; shell_filled_emphasis is reserved for emphasis states; status_semantic icons pair with a semantic_status value; file_type_rich icons MAY carry more color and detail but never impersonate shell chrome; illustration_* treatments are reserved for onboarding, empty states, and documentation.

semantic_status_class.description: Frozen semantic-status vocabulary for product-wide status messaging. Maps to the five state palettes in the style guide: success / warning / danger / info / insight. A surface that introduces a sixth palette without a schema bump is non-conforming; status conveyed by color alone is also non-conforming.

trust_visual_state_class.description: Frozen trust / durable-attention visual-state vocabulary. Every trust- or attention-bearing surface maps exactly one of these states per addressable slot. Brand gold is NEVER used to communicate restricted or policy-locked state. quiet_hours_active is the held-notification posture; policy_locked is the admin-policy narrowing posture.

source_of_truth_class.description: Frozen source-of-truth class for a design-token / component-state / theme-support row. Every manifest names exactly one canonical source; copy-only shadow registries are non-conforming.

audit_event_id.description: Audit-event ids on the design_token_vocabulary audit stream. Events MUST NOT carry raw asset bytes, raw screenshots, or raw rendered imagery.

denial_reason.description: Denial-reason vocabulary on the design_token_vocabulary audit stream. Denials fail closed; silent downgrade to a generic 'styling issue' is forbidden.

hex_color_literal.description: Hex color literal: 3, 4, 6, or 8 hex digits (with or without alpha). Surfaces consume tokens; hex literals are carried here for seed fidelity only.

rgba_color_literal.description: CSS rgba() color literal. Surfaces consume tokens; rgba literals are carried here for seed fidelity only.

color_literal.description: Any frozen color literal accepted by the export: a hex or an rgba() string. The style guide is the source of truth for human-facing presentation; this schema preserves the raw value so later conformance can diff exact bytes.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch the row was minted against. Re-exported from ADR-0008 without modification.

trust_state.description: Workspace / runtime trust state the row was minted against. restricted and policy_locked MUST map to restricted_workspace / policy_locked trust_visual_state values; they MUST NOT be conveyed by color alone.

execution_context_id.description: Optional execution-context id. Re-exported from ADR-0009 without modification.

policy_context.description: Re-exported policy-context packet (ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 / ADR-0001). Present on every record so later conformance audits can attribute a row to a specific policy epoch / trust / execution context.

redaction_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json (ADR-0011). The token-export stream never carries raw asset bytes or raw screenshots; metadata_safe_default is the usual value.

source_doc_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the canonical doc or registry for this row (e.g. 'docs/design/design_token_component_state_vocabulary.md' or '.t2/docs/Aureline_UX_Design_System_Style_Guide.md#7.2').

source_revision_ref.description: Optional opaque revision id so conformance can pin the source state.

source_of_truth_ref.description: Pointer to the canonical source-of-truth for this row. Surfaces quote this ref; they MUST NOT re-mint the row's value locally.

token_family_ids.description: Stable ids of every token_family_record published in this manifest. One id per frozen token_family_class.

component_state_ids.description: Stable ids of every component_state_record published in this manifest. One id per frozen component_state_class.

theme_support_row_ids.description: Stable ids of every theme_support_row_record. The M0 seed publishes one row per frozen theme_class.

accessibility_posture_ids.description: Stable ids of every accessibility_posture_record. The M0 seed publishes one row per frozen accessibility_posture_class.

layer_order_ids.description: Stable ids of every layer_order_record. The M0 seed publishes one row per frozen layer_order_class.

scrim_token_ids.description: Stable ids of every scrim_token_record. The M0 seed publishes one row per frozen scrim_class.

running_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque ref to the running build identity the manifest was minted against (ADR-0013 exact-build identity re-exported without modification).

design_token_export_manifest_record.description: Envelope for a single design-token vocabulary export. Every consumer (shell, docs / help, trust, onboarding, durable-attention, conformance tooling) reads the manifest and then resolves the per-entity records by id.

token_namespace.description: The dotted / hyphenated namespace the family lives under (e.g. 'al.color.bg', 'space', 'type', 'z', 'motion'). Every token in the family MUST begin with this namespace; bare tokens without the namespace are non-conforming.

token_count.description: Count of tokens in the family at mint time. Later conformance diffs count against the implementation.

example_token_names.description: A small, illustrative list of concrete token names in this family. Seed value; not the authoritative full list.

short_description.description: One-sentence description of the family's intent.

token_family_record.description: One record per frozen token_family_class. Surfaces reference tokens by name within the namespace; the manifest seeds the canonical set so later conformance gates can diff implementation against this list.

applies_to_surfaces.description: Surfaces on which this state is expected to render at M0. The enum re-exports the shell_interaction_safety_contract surface_class and adds onboarding / notification / inspector surfaces that are addressed by this vocabulary but not by the interaction-safety contract.

paired_with_semantic_status.description: Optional semantic_status values the state is usually paired with (e.g. disabled often pairs with warning copy; degraded pairs with warning or info). Surfaces MUST NOT convey the state by color alone regardless of this pairing.

not_color_alone.description: Frozen at true: every state MUST be conveyed by more than color (shape, border, icon, text). Included explicitly so later conformance gates can diff against a simple rule.

short_description.description: One-sentence description of when this state applies.

component_state_record.description: One record per frozen component_state_class. Every styled interactive surface maps its local states back to this set; combining states (e.g. focus + selected) is permitted, minting new names is non-conforming.

is_reference_theme.description: True only for dark_reference at M0; light_parity and the two high-contrast rows MUST be parity-quality, not lesser modes.

is_first_party.description: Frozen at true: all four theme rows are first-party. High contrast is NEVER a third-party afterthought.

is_color_alone_prohibited.description: Frozen at true: no theme may rely on hue alone for state / status meaning. Later conformance diffs assert this rule against every theme row.

token_overrides_namespace.description: Namespace under which this theme ships its semantic-theme token overrides (e.g. 'al.color' for the four core themes). The style guide is the source of truth for concrete values; this row reserves the namespace.

minimum_text_contrast_target.description: Minimum normal-text contrast target (e.g. 4.5 for AA, 7 for high-contrast reference). Seed value; exact per-token contrast lives in the style guide.

minimum_ui_contrast_target.description: Minimum UI / icon / boundary contrast target (e.g. 3 for AA, 4.5 for high-contrast reference).

focus_ring_contrast_target.description: Minimum focus-ring contrast target (e.g. 3 for AA).

focus_ring_stroke_px.description: Minimum focus-ring stroke width in logical pixels. Seed value; style guide pins 2 px.

forced_colors_friendly.description: Whether the theme is expected to defer to OS 'forced colors' / system high-contrast overrides when the platform reports them. Frozen true for high_contrast_* rows; recommended true for others.

example_semantic_tokens.description: A small, illustrative set of semantic token values for this theme. Seed fidelity; exhaustive per-token table lives in the style guide.

theme_support_row_record.description: One record per frozen theme_class. Shell, docs / help, trust, onboarding, and durable-attention surfaces resolve the active theme row before rendering; admin policy MAY narrow the exposed set, it MAY NOT introduce a fifth theme without a schema bump.

suppresses_motion_families.description: Motion families suppressed or strongly reduced in this posture. Re-exported from UI / UX Spec §10.4 Motion restrictions and the style guide's motion-restriction row.

preserves_focus_visibility.description: Frozen at true: every accessibility posture MUST preserve focus visibility. An implementation that hides focus rings in a low-power or reduced-motion posture is non-conforming.

preserves_state_conveyance.description: Frozen at true: every accessibility posture MUST preserve state conveyance (shape, border, icon, text). A posture that strips all state chrome and leaves only color is non-conforming.

engagement_cue.description: How the posture becomes visible to the user when engaged (e.g. status-item pill for motion_power_saver, settings-surface label for motion_low_motion). Seed value; exact copy lives in the style guide.

allowed_duration_tokens.description: Subset of frozen motion duration tokens permitted in this posture. motion_reduced / motion_low_motion / motion_power_saver typically collapse to motion.instant for non-essential motion; motion_critical_hot_path forces motion.instant regardless of user preference.

accessibility_posture_record.description: One record per frozen accessibility_posture_class. A surface MAY escalate to a more restrictive posture (e.g. motion_standard -> motion_power_saver when the device enters thermal pressure); it MAY NOT silently downgrade.

ordinal.description: Relative layer ordinal. z_base is 0; z_critical is 6. Extensions and local features consume the layer token, never the ordinal.

is_extension_consumable.description: Whether extensions and local features may stack content at this layer. Frozen false for z_critical (reserved for trust / auth / security-critical overlays).

dialog_and_critical_outrank.description: Frozen at true: dialogs and security-critical sheets always render above menus and transient hovers.

toast_never_blocks_dialog.description: Frozen at true: toasts never obscure the primary action area of an active blocking dialog.

layer_order_record.description: One record per frozen layer_order_class. Surfaces consume the token; hard-coded z-index values are non-conforming.

alpha.description: Alpha (opacity) value for this scrim token. 0 for scrim_none; 0.4 for scrim_weak; 0.56 for scrim_strong; overlay_dim_presentation carries a smaller alpha so keyboard focus and active-region cues remain visible.

never_sole_state_indicator.description: Frozen at true: reduced opacity is NEVER the only indicator of a state (disabled, blocked, loading, held). Later conformance asserts this against every scrim token.

preserves_focus_visibility.description: Frozen at true: every scrim preserves focus rings on the underlying surface; decorative dimming MUST NOT obscure the focused interactive control.

scrim_token_record.description: One record per frozen scrim_class. Scrims communicate depth and focus, not state.

subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the subject of the event (a manifest id, family id, state id, theme row id, posture id, layer id, scrim id, or conformance-diff id). Raw asset bytes, raw screenshots, and raw rendered imagery never appear here.

token_export_audit_event_record.description: One event per emission, refusal, addition, posture engagement, schema-version bump, or conformance-diff on the design_token_vocabulary audit stream.
